questions.question: What makes the latch permanent once flipped?
questions.expected_answer: Two connected gear bits form a latch that cannot flip back once activated
hints: Gear bits connected together cannot be flipped back to original position

questions.question: What happens to the first blue ball that hits the latch?
questions.expected_answer: It flips the latch and triggers the right lever
hints: The first blue ball activates the latch mechanism

questions.question: After the latch is flipped, where are all subsequent balls routed?
hints: The flipped latch routes all balls to the left lever

questions.question: Trace the state of the gear bits as balls pass through
questions.expected_answer: Start: GB1=right, GB2=right; Ball 1: GB1 flips to left (latch activates); Subsequent balls: latch stays in flipped position
hints: Once flipped, the latch remains in that position permanently
